data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260514T155540-0f695a5e/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260514T155540-0f695a5e/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260514T155540-0f695a5e/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260514T155540-0f695a5e/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260514T155540-0f695a5e/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-4e9f610f">
    <title>try: attach prose-check evidence for docs-changing attempts</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
DDx should run prose checks during normal docs-changing attempts without users reminding the agent. Findings need to be captured as evidence and fed back while the attempt is still editable.

ROOT CAUSE
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-06-prose-quality-integration.md:264-283 requires `ddx try` / `ddx work` attempts that change Markdown under `docs/` to run `ddx doc prose --changed`, attach evidence, and keep prose advisory. No workflow hook currently implements that contract.

SMART JUSTIFICATION: Workflow-hook policy is high-judgment because it touches execution finalization and evidence. A weak implementation could block correct work on advisory prose findings or run too late for agents to repair obvious prose issues.

PROPOSED FIX
- Detect docs Markdown changes in `ddx try` / `ddx work` attempts.
- Run `ddx doc prose --changed` before finalization when docs changed.
- Attach prose-check evidence to the attempt.
- Feed findings back to the agent before final response when editable.
- Keep prose findings advisory by default and separate from correctness review.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not make prose checks mandatory blockers.
- Do not run full-doc cleanup.
- Do not alter Fizeau routing.

DEPS
Depends on the Vale-backed command and the upgraded writing skill.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. `TestTryDocsChanged_AttachesProseEvidence` covers docs-changing attempts.
2. `TestTryNoDocsChanged_SkipsProseCheck` covers non-doc attempts.
3. `TestTryProseFindings_AdvisoryByDefault` proves findings do not block closure.
4. `TestTryProseFindings_FedBackBeforeFinalization` covers editable feedback timing.
5. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>adr:025, area:docprose, area:execution, kind:integration, phase:2, prose-quality, spec:PLAN-2026-05-06-prose-quality-integration, tier:smart</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-ccda7a32" spec-id="PLAN-2026-05-06-prose-quality-integration" base-rev="b431816a12140fe80241d13d336f00efbf3f78ec" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260514T155540-0f695a5e"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="PLAN-2026-05-06-prose-quality-integration" path="docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-06-prose-quality-integration.md">Plan: Prose Quality Integration with Vale</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Commit landed cleanly with all pre-commit checks green.

Summary of what was added for `ddx-4e9f610f`:

- **`cli/internal/agent/prose_evidence.go`** — `AttachProseEvidence` + `ProseEvidenceHook` type. Detects `docs/**/*.md` changes between BaseRev and ResultRev (via `git diff --name-only`), runs the embedded docprose checker on each file at ResultRev (via `git show`), writes findings JSON to `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/prose-findings.json`, and appends an advisory `prose-check.findings` bead event. Both detectors are injectable for tests.
- **`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`** — new `ProseEvidenceHook` field on `ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime`; invoked in the success branch just before `CloseWithEvidence` so findings land before finalization.
- **`cli/cmd/try.go` + `cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go`** — wire the hook with `ddx try` / `ddx work` source labels; default uses real git+docprose, overridable via `f.proseEvidenceHookOverride`.
- **`cli/cmd/command_factory.go`** — new `proseEvidenceHookOverride` test seam.
- **`cli/cmd/try_prose_test.go`** — the four named acceptance tests:
  - `TestTryDocsChanged_AttachesProseEvidence`
  - `TestTryNoDocsChanged_SkipsProseCheck`
  - `TestTryProseFindings_AdvisoryByDefault`
  - `TestTryProseFindings_FedBackBeforeFinalization`
